body:
## Summary

Closes #98.

**Primary bug:** JavaScript release tag pages showed a "404: badge not found" SVG instead of a version-specific npm badge. `scripts/format-release-notes.mjs` was called with the full tag (for example `js-v1.7.12`), stripped only a leading `v`, and interpolated the rest directly into `https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-<tag>-blue.svg`. shields.io's `/badge/label-message-color` path uses `-` as a field separator, so the extra hyphens in `js-v1.7.12` broke the URL.

**Also addressed:**
- Release titles now read `[JavaScript] v<version>` / `[Rust] v<version>` instead of `JS v<version>` / `Rust v<version>`, as requested in the issue.
- All 12 already-published releases (4 broken JS + 8 Rust) have been repaired in place via a new one-shot script, so the GitHub Releases page no longer shows any broken badges or pre-fix titles.

## Root cause

`scripts/format-release-notes.mjs:191` (before):
```js
const versionWithoutV = version.replace(/^v/, '');
```

With `version === 'js-v1.7.12'`, `versionWithoutV` remained `js-v1.7.12`, producing `/badge/npm-js-v1.7.12-blue.svg`. shields.io responded with an SVG whose text read `404: badge not found`.

Fix:
```js
const versionWithoutV = version.replace(/^[a-z]+-/i, '').replace(/^v/i, '');
```

## Before / after

Broken badge (`/badge/npm-js-v1.7.12-blue.svg`): renders `404: badge not found`.

![before](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/blob/issue-98-c765b5595cf4/docs/case-studies/issue-98/reference/broken-badge-before.svg?raw=true)

Fixed badge (`/badge/npm-1.7.12-blue.svg`): renders `npm: 1.7.12`.

![after](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/blob/issue-98-c765b5595cf4/docs/case-studies/issue-98/reference/fixed-badge-after.svg?raw=true)

Live release after the retroactive repair: https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/releases/tag/js-v1.7.12

## Changes

| Commit | What |
| --- | --- |
| `fix(release): strip language prefix from shields.io badge URL` | `scripts/format-release-notes.mjs` normalizes `js-v<version>` / `rust-v<version>` / `v<version>` to a bare semver before interpolation. Adds `experiments/test-release-badge-encoding.mjs` as a standalone regression test. |
| `feat(release): use [JavaScript] and [Rust] prefixes in release titles` | `scripts/create-github-release.mjs` and `scripts/rust-create-github-release.mjs` now publish `[JavaScript] v<version>` / `[Rust] v<version>`. |
| `chore(release): add one-shot repair for already-published release bodies` | Idempotent repair script `scripts/fix-existing-release-badges.mjs`. Supports `--dry-run`. Already run once against the repo's 12 pre-fix releases. |
| `docs(case-study): document issue #98 badge investigation` | `docs/case-studies/issue-98/` with timeline, requirements table, root cause, preserved data, verification steps, and upstream follow-up notes. |

## Upstream follow-up

`link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` ships the same latent `replace(/^v/, '')` logic. The template repo itself does not use a `js-v` tag prefix, so the bug does not manifest there - but downstream consumers that tag per-language (like this repo) will hit it. The `link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template` uses the dynamic `/crates/v/<name>` shields endpoint and sidesteps the issue entirely. Plan and reproduction steps for an upstream report are documented in `docs/case-studies/issue-98/README.md`.

## Test plan

- [x] Regression test: `node experiments/test-release-badge-encoding.mjs` passes.
- [x] `cd js && npm run lint` reports 0 errors (only pre-existing warnings).
- [x] `cd js && npm run format:check` is clean.
- [x] All modified scripts pass `node --check` syntax validation.
- [x] Dry-run of `scripts/fix-existing-release-badges.mjs` lists exactly the 12 releases that need updating.
- [x] After running the repair script live: `curl -sS https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-1.7.12-blue.svg` renders `aria-label="npm: 1.7.12"`; `gh release view js-v1.7.12` returns `"name":"[JavaScript] v1.7.12"` and a fixed badge URL.

title: Fix broken JS release version-specific badge and normalize release titles
